ranked_movies.title: Melancholia
ranked_movies.reason: Explicitly recommended multiple times as a prime example of a film about depression and hopelessness. It is the most frequently mentioned movie in the discussions.

ranked_movies.title: Requiem for a Dream
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a quintessential and deeply depressing film from beginning to end. A classic example often brought up in these contexts.

ranked_movies.title: The Master
ranked_movies.reason: Heavily discussed for its portrayal of mental illness and depression, specifically Joaquin Phoenix's character, Freddie Quell. A focal point of a dedicated thread.

ranked_movies.title: Manchester by the Sea
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a film where the protagonist is depressed, miserable, and hopeless. A well-known modern drama fitting the theme.

ranked_movies.title: Lars and the Real Girl
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in a list of films about depressed protagonists. While noted to have a sweet ending, its inclusion suggests it deals heavily with themes of loneliness and mental health.

ranked_movies.title: Synecdoche, New York
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a film that feels profoundly depressing. Its complex and bleak narrative makes it a standout in this category.

ranked_movies.title: Her
ranked_movies.reason: Included in a list of films featuring depressed or lonely protagonists. Explores themes of isolation and connection in a modern world.

ranked_movies.title: Inside Llewyn Davis
ranked_movies.reason: Specifically recommended as a movie about a depressed and hopeless protagonist. Its melancholic tone is a key feature.

ranked_movies.title: Three Colors: Blue
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a film dealing with grief and depression. Part of a renowned trilogy known for its emotional depth.

ranked_movies.title: The Whale
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a film about a depressed and miserable protagonist. Its subject matter directly deals with isolation and despair.

ranked_movies.title: Belladonna of Sadness
ranked_movies.reason: Described as an animation that is literally 'if depression was an animation.' A unique and highly recommended entry for its artistic portrayal of despair.

ranked_movies.title: Drive
ranked_movies.reason: Listed among films with a depressed protagonist. Known for its stoic and emotionally damaged main character.

ranked_movies.title: Wendy and Lucy
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a film that feels hopeless and depressing. A minimalist story focusing on struggle and isolation.

ranked_movies.title: On the Count of Three
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a movie about depression and suicide. A dark comedy that deals directly with hopelessness.

ranked_movies.title: Harold and Maude
ranked_movies.reason: A classic film mentioned in the context of depression and morbid themes, though it ultimately has a life-affirming message.

ranked_movies.title: Little Miss Sunshine
ranked_movies.reason: Included in a list of films about dysfunctional and depressed characters. While ultimately uplifting, its characters grapple with deep personal despair.

ranked_movies.title: Schindler's List
ranked_movies.reason: Cited in an AskReddit thread as a movie that is profoundly depressing from beginning to end due to its historical subject matter.

ranked_movies.title: What Dreams May Come
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as an extremely depressing film, dealing with themes of death, the afterlife, and despair.

ranked_movies.title: Incendies
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a brutally depressing film. Its shocking narrative and tragic revelations make it a powerful entry.

ranked_movies.title: I Stand Alone
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a relentlessly bleak and depressing film. Known for its gritty and nihilistic portrayal of its protagonist.
